texto: The nuns taught us there are two ways through life, the way of Nature and the way of Grace. You have to choose which one you'll follow. Grace doesn't try to please itself. Accepts being slighted, forgotten, disliked. Accepts insults and injuries.Nature only wants to please itself. Get others to please it too. Likes to lord it over them. To have its own way. It finds reasons to be unhappy when all the world is shining around it. And love is smiling through all things.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Terrence Malick sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “The nuns taught us there are two ways through life, the way of Nature and the way of Grace. You h…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a felicidade, grace, love, útil em momentos de love.

texto: I film quite a bit of footage, then edit. Changes before your eyes, things you can do and things you can't. My attitude is always 'let it keep rolling.'
explicacao: Citação de Terrence Malick sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I film quite a bit of footage, then edit. Changes before your eyes, things you can do and things …” articula reflexao, eyes, changes em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e changes.
